title: Torture by French army still haunts Algerians: 'France made us all suffer too much'
subhead: 'Algeria's unhealed war memories' (Part 1/3): Survivors of abuses committed by the French military during the Algerian War of Independence tell their stories and those of their relatives, and seem reluctant to the idea of reconciliation with France.

title: Trump says canceling Iran strikes amid possible deal, but Iran says no 'final conclusion'
France demography: The upcoming political time bomb
2026 World Cup: A climate disaster in the making
subhead: The upcoming World Cup is set to be the most polluting in history, multiplying both the number of matches and the distances teams and fans must travel from one stadium to another. FIFA is embracing this irresponsible approach, which risks harming both players and the sport itself.

title: Lyhanna case: suspect Jérôme Barella was flagged to French police by a US anti-child abuse organization
subhead: Suspicious online activity by the man suspected of killing Lyhanna was reported by NCMEC, a leading organization that aggregates alerts collected by internet service providers, major web platforms and social media companies.

title: Israel bars RFI journalist from entering West Bank and increases pressure on foreign press
subhead: The expulsion of Alice Froussard, a journalist for Radio France Internationale (RFI), comes amid severe violence in the occupied territory and a total closure of the Gaza Strip to international media, where more than 260 Palestinian journalists have been killed since the attack of October 7, 2023.

title: Germany's far right wages culture war against the Bauhaus
subhead: The legendary architecture school marks its centenary amid political and identity tensions, as the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party surges in Saxony-Anhalt. The Bauhaus Foundation in Dessau fears budget cuts and renewed attacks on its modernist legacy.

'While the G7 is indisputably obsolescent, nothing seems capable of replacing it'
subhead: Although rooted in a bygone era and battered by Donald Trump, the Group of Seven continues to meet purely out of habit, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris, one week before the summit chaired by French President Emmanuel Macron in Evian.

title: The murky ties between FIFA and the US
subhead: After the 2022 World Cup was awarded to Qatar, the US launched a legal offensive against several FIFA members before securing the co-hosting of the 2026 tournament. This is the story of the rise of American soft power in global football.

title: Bardella's contradiction of Le Pen on pensions confounds French far right
subhead: Jordan Bardella, who would stand as the Rassemblement National's presidential candidate if Marine Le Pen's election ban is upheld, has broken with his boss's line on a crucial policy issue.
